description: The lower surface of a room on which one walks; the prepared ground inside. 'Tomae' names what supports standing—the level surface of habitation.
sound_symbolism: The word grounds with contact. 'To' touches with feet meeting surface. 'Ma' stabilizes with solid foundation. Then 'e' extends flat and level, floor spreading.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'tomae' (the floor). Works with 'moru' (wall) and 'toru' (roof) to form shelter. The horizontal below.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Ground of practice—the floor we sit on, walk on, the support for daily life.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Floor as foundation—the prepared surface separating dweller from bare earth.
